title: Project Skeleton
deliverable: `npm run dev` renders a styled empty shell: deep emerald dark gradient background, centred ~480 px container, the 'niyet' wordmark, and all CSS variables for the theme palette defined. No interactive behaviour.

in: Scaffold Vite + React app with `npm create vite@latest niyet -- --template react`
Remove all Vite boilerplate content (Counter, App.css demo, logo, HelloWorld markup)
Define CSS custom properties (variables) for the full theme palette on :root in index.css
Deep emerald dark gradient background applied to body/root
Centred container div, max-width ~480 px, vertically and horizontally centred on viewport
Display the 'niyet' wordmark — Turkish word rendered in a gold accent colour, large calligraphic-style font (system or Google Fonts fallback)
Sub-tagline text in Turkish beneath the wordmark, muted tone
Base typography: font-family, line-height, anti-aliasing on :root
Folder structure stubs: src/components/, src/hooks/, src/lib/ — each with an empty placeholder .js or .jsx file so Git tracks the directory
placeholder files: src/components/.gitkeep OR minimal barrel index.js files, same for hooks/ and lib/
ESLint and Vite config left at defaults (no modifications needed beyond scaffold)
out: Any queue, task, or chain logic
Supabase client initialisation or any network calls
Exact final hex values for brand colours (placeholders accepted, names must match PRD intent)
Google Fonts network import (can use system font stack as placeholder)

files.purpose: Update <title> to 'Niyet', set lang='tr', link any Google Font if chosen (optional at this block)
Define all CSS custom properties on :root (colours, spacing, typography scale, radius, transitions). Apply background gradient and base typography to body.
Strip Vite boilerplate. Render only the centred container with the wordmark and tagline. Import index.css. No state, no logic.
Keep as-is from scaffold (mounts App into #root). Remove React.StrictMode only if it causes issues — leave it by default.
Placeholder stub — exports empty default component. Marks the file for Block 2.
Placeholder stub — exports empty default component.
Placeholder stub — exports empty default component.
Placeholder stub — exports empty default component.
Placeholder stub — exports empty default component.
Placeholder stub — exports empty custom hook skeleton.
Placeholder stub — exports empty custom hook skeleton.
Placeholder stub — exports empty object or TODO comment. Will hold localStorage layer in Block 14.
Placeholder stub — exports empty object. Will hold Supabase push/pull logic in Block 18.
Placeholder stub — exports empty `playTickSound` function. Will be implemented in Block 5.
Document required environment variables VITE_SUPABASE_URL and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY without real values. Committed to repo.
Ensure .env (with real secrets) is ignored. Vite scaffold already includes node_modules and dist.

implementation_steps.details: From the parent directory (not inside niyet/), run: `npm create vite@latest niyet -- --template react`. When prompted, confirm. Then `cd niyet && npm install`. Verify `npm run dev` opens http://localhost:5173 with the default Vite welcome page.
Inside niyet/, run `git init && git add . && git commit -m 'chore: scaffold vite react app'`. This creates the initial commit so subsequent blocks produce clean diffs.

Create `.env.example` at repo root with two lines: `VITE_SUPABASE_URL=` and `VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=`. Open `.gitignore` and confirm it contains `.env` (Vite scaffold does this by default; add it if absent). Do NOT create a real `.env` file yet — that belongs to Block 16.

Open `index.html`. Change `<title>Vite + React</title>` to `<title>Niyet</title>`. Set `<html lang="tr">`. Optionally add `<meta name="description" content="Niyetini belirle, bir adım at.">`. Leave the `<script type="module" src="/src/main.jsx">` line unchanged.

implementation_steps.description: Define CSS custom properties in index.css
implementation_steps.details: Replace the entire contents of `src/index.css` with a `:root {}` block defining the following CSS variable names (exact names are canonical; hex values are placeholder — use the closest reasonable approximation and mark with a comment TODO: finalise hex): `--color-bg-start` (dark emerald, e.g. #0a2e1c placeholder), `--color-bg-end` (slightly deeper/shifted emerald, e.g. #061a10 placeholder), `--color-surface` (card/container surface, semi-transparent emerald, e.g. rgba(10,60,30,0.7) placeholder), `--color-surface-raised` (slightly lighter surface), `--color-border` (subtle border, e.g. rgba(255,255,255,0.08)), `--color-text-primary` (near-white, e.g. #f0ede6), `--color-text-secondary` (muted warm white, e.g. #a89f8c), `--color-text-muted` (very muted, for done log strikethrough etc.), `--color-gold` (calligraphy/accent gold, e.g. #c9a84c placeholder), `--color-gold-dim` (dimmer gold for secondary use), `--color-gold-glow` (gold with opacity for glow effects, e.g. rgba(201,168,76,0.4)), `--color-emerald-accent` (brighter emerald for active states, e.g. #2ecc71 placeholder), `--color-danger` (red for destructive actions, e.g. #e74c3c), `--font-family-base` ('Segoe UI', system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif), `--font-family-display` (same as base for now; will be replaced with calligraphic font if chosen), `--font-size-xs` (0.75rem), `--font-size-sm` (0.875rem), `--font-size-base` (1rem), `--font-size-lg` (1.25rem), `--font-size-xl` (1.5rem), `--font-size-2xl` (2rem), `--font-size-3xl` (3rem), `--font-weight-normal` (400), `--font-weight-medium` (500), `--font-weight-bold` (700), `--line-height-tight` (1.2), `--line-height-base` (1.6), `--spacing-xs` (0.25rem), `--spacing-sm` (0.5rem), `--spacing-md` (1rem), `--spacing-lg` (1.5rem), `--spacing-xl` (2rem), `--spacing-2xl` (3rem), `--radius-sm` (6px), `--radius-md` (12px), `--radius-lg` (20px), `--radius-full` (9999px), `--transition-fast` (150ms ease), `--transition-base` (300ms ease), `--transition-slow` (500ms cubic-bezier(.4,0,.2,1)), `--container-max-width` (480px), `--shadow-card` (0 4px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.4)).

implementation_steps.description: Apply background and base typography to body in index.css
implementation_steps.details: After the :root block, add: `*, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; margin: 0; padding: 0; }`. Then `body { background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--color-bg-start) 0%, var(--color-bg-end) 100%); background-attachment: fixed; min-height: 100vh; color: var(--color-text-primary); font-family: var(--font-family-base); font-size: var(--font-size-base); line-height: var(--line-height-base); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; }`. Also add `#root { min-height: 100vh; display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; }`.

implementation_steps.description: Strip Vite boilerplate from App.jsx and render the wordmark shell
implementation_steps.details:
Open `src/App.jsx`. Delete everything. Write a minimal functional component. Import `'./index.css'` at the top (if not already done in main.jsx — confirm main.jsx imports it; if so, no duplicate needed). The component returns JSX with: an outer `
` with inline style `{ width: '100%', maxWidth: 'var(--container-max-width)', padding: 'var(--spacing-xl)', display: 'flex', flexDirection: 'column', alignItems: 'center', gap: 'var(--spacing-md)' }`. Inside: (a) an `

` for the wordmark with text `niyet` (lowercase intentional), inline style `{ fontSize: 'var(--font-size-3xl)', fontWeight: 'var(--font-weight-bold)', color: 'var(--color-gold)', letterSpacing: '0.05em', fontFamily: 'var(--font-family-display)' }`. (b) a `

` tagline with text `Bedeninin atacağı bir sonraki adımı gösterir.`, inline style `{ fontSize: 'var(--font-size-sm)', color: 'var(--color-text-secondary)', textAlign: 'center' }`. Export default the component.

Open `src/main.jsx`. It should import React, ReactDOM, App, and index.css, then call `ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')).render(<App />)`. The Vite scaffold produces this exactly. Make no changes unless something was accidentally modified.

Delete `src/App.css` (replaced by index.css CSS variables). Delete `src/assets/react.svg`. Delete `public/vite.svg`. These files are no longer referenced after App.jsx cleanup. Confirm no import errors in the terminal after deletion.

implementation_steps.description: Create folder structure stubs for components/
implementation_steps.details: Create the following files with minimal stub content. For .jsx component stubs, use: `export default function ComponentName() { return null; }` — replace ComponentName with the actual name. Create: `src/components/Queue.jsx`, `src/components/AddSteps.jsx`, `src/components/Chains.jsx`, `src/components/ChainEdit.jsx`, `src/components/CelebrationBurst.jsx`.

implementation_steps.description: Create folder structure stubs for hooks/
implementation_steps.details: Create: `src/hooks/useQueue.js` with content `export function useQueue() { return {}; }`. Create: `src/hooks/useChains.js` with content `export function useChains() { return {}; }`. These are placeholder skeletons — return values will be defined in later blocks.

implementation_steps.description: Create folder structure stubs for lib/
implementation_steps.details:
Create: `src/lib/storage.js` with content `// TODO Block 14: localStorage source-of-truth layer
export const storage = {};`. Create: `src/lib/sync.js` with content `// TODO Block 18: Supabase push/pull and merge-on-login
export const sync = {};`. Create: `src/lib/audio.js` with content `// TODO Block 5: Web Audio API three-oscillator tick sound
export function playTickSound() {}`.

implementation_steps.description: Run dev server and visually verify the skeleton
implementation_steps.details: Run `npm run dev`. Open http://localhost:5173. Confirm: (1) page background is a dark emerald gradient, (2) the word 'niyet' appears centred in gold, (3) the Turkish tagline appears below in muted text, (4) no console errors, (5) no Vite boilerplate (counter, logos) visible. If the background appears white, confirm that index.css is imported in main.jsx and that the `body` rule is present.

implementation_steps.description: Verify build output has no errors
implementation_steps.details: Run `npm run build`. Confirm it exits with code 0 and produces a `dist/` folder. The dist folder is not committed (it is in .gitignore). This ensures no type/import errors introduced by the stub files.

data_structures.name: CSSCustomProperties
data_structures.notes: All hex values are placeholders at this block. Variable names are canonical and must not change in later blocks — only values are updated. Every component in subsequent blocks uses these variables via inline styles or CSS class references.

interfaces.name: App
interfaces.purpose: Root component. At this block renders only the wordmark shell. In Block 2+ will hold view-router state ('main'|'add'|'chains'|'chain-edit'|'auth') and auth state.

interfaces.name: playTickSound
interfaces.purpose: Stub at this block. Will be implemented in Block 5 using Web Audio API with three oscillator layers.

interfaces.name: useQueue
interfaces.purpose: Stub at this block. Will expose queue CRUD operations over the localStorage layer in Block 14.

interfaces.name: useChains
interfaces.purpose: Stub at this block. Will expose chains CRUD and default seed logic in Block 14.

acceptance_criteria: `npm run dev` starts without errors and the page loads at http://localhost:5173
Page background is a dark emerald gradient (not white, not black, not browser default grey)
The word 'niyet' is visible, centred, in a gold/amber colour, large font
The Turkish tagline 'Bedeninin atacağı bir sonraki adımı gösterir.' is visible beneath the wordmark
No Vite boilerplate content is visible (no counter, no Vite/React logos, no 'Edit src/App.jsx' text)
No JavaScript console errors or warnings on initial load
All 5 component stubs exist under src/components/ and export a default function
Both hook stubs exist under src/hooks/ and export named functions
All 3 lib stubs exist under src/lib/ with the correct export names (storage, sync, playTickSound)
.env.example exists and contains VITE_SUPABASE_URL= and VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= (no real values)
index.css :root block contains all CSS variable names listed in implementation step 1.5
The container is visually centred on the viewport on a 375 px wide viewport (mobile simulation)
verification: Run `npm run dev` and open browser at http://localhost:5173 — confirm dark emerald gradient fills the viewport
Open DevTools → Elements → inspect `<html>` → confirm `lang='tr'` and `<title>Niyet</title>`
Open DevTools → Elements → inspect `:root` computed styles — confirm all --color-* and --font-* CSS variables are present
Open DevTools → Console tab — confirm zero errors and zero warnings on page load
Resize browser to 375 px width — confirm wordmark and tagline remain centred and do not overflow
Run `npm run build` in terminal — confirm exit code 0 and presence of dist/index.html
Run `ls src/components/` — confirm Queue.jsx, AddSteps.jsx, Chains.jsx, ChainEdit.jsx, CelebrationBurst.jsx all exist
Run `ls src/hooks/` — confirm useQueue.js and useChains.js exist
Run `ls src/lib/` — confirm storage.js, sync.js, audio.js exist
Open src/lib/audio.js — confirm it exports `playTickSound` function (even if empty body)
Confirm src/App.css does NOT exist (deleted in step 1.9)

questionable_decisions.decision: index.css is used for global :root CSS variables and body styles; all component styles will use inline styles (per PRD §4.1)
questionable_decisions.assumption: Inline styles referencing CSS variables via `var(--name)` work in all target browsers and are sufficient for the full design system without a CSS module or styled-components approach
alternatives: CSS Modules per component (scoped, no global pollution, but extra files)
A single stylesheet with class selectors (familiar, but conflicts with PRD 'inline styles' directive)
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Keep inline styles + CSS variables as specified in PRD. This is a deliberate PRD constraint, not a default choice. Accept the occasional verbosity in exchange for zero build-step styling.

questionable_decisions.decision: Google Fonts import is excluded from Block 1 (system font stack used as placeholder for --font-family-display)
questionable_decisions.assumption: The exact calligraphic font is deferred per PRD §5.1; using a system font at skeleton stage avoids committing to a font choice before the design is validated against live screens
alternatives: Import a placeholder Arabic/calligraphic Google Font now (e.g. Amiri, Scheherazade) to get a closer feel
Use a local variable font if one is available on the system
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Import a temporary Google Font (e.g. 'Cinzel' for the wordmark, as a Latin calligraphic placeholder) to validate the visual feel early. Swap at the design finalisation block. This adds one network request but unblocks design review.

questionable_decisions.decision: Placeholder hex values are committed for all CSS colour variables rather than leaving variables undefined
questionable_decisions.assumption: Having approximate placeholder values allows all subsequent blocks to render with a consistent look-and-feel during development; final values are refined in a dedicated design-polish block
alternatives: Leave colour vars as empty strings until a design block finalises them (would break visuals in every block)
Define a DESIGN_TOKENS constant in JS and derive CSS vars from it at runtime (adds complexity, no benefit at this stage)
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Use placeholder hex values as specified. Mark each with a `/* TODO: finalise */` comment. This is the correct trade-off — renderable placeholders beat invisible styles.

questionable_decisions.decision: Component stubs export `return null` rather than a skeleton with placeholder HTML
questionable_decisions.assumption: Block 1 explicitly excludes any interactive or structural content; null-returning stubs are the smallest valid React component
alternatives:
Export a div with a Turkish placeholder comment inside each component (e.g.
{/* Kuyruk — Block 2 */}
)
Use throw new Error('not implemented') to make premature usage loudly fail
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Use `return null` for components and empty object returns for hooks. Clean, no phantom DOM nodes, and consistent. A TODO comment in the file is sufficient documentation.

questionable_decisions.decision: No routing library (React Router etc.) installed at skeleton stage
questionable_decisions.assumption: PRD §6 explicitly states 'No routing library needed for v1' — view state will be held in React useState in App.jsx. Installing a router now would create unused dependencies and contradict the PRD.
alternatives: Install React Router v6 now to future-proof (adds ~50 KB, complexity, contradicts PRD)
Use a hash-based manual router pattern
questionable_decisions.recommendation: Confirm no router at this block. If in Block 2 the view-state approach feels unwieldy, revisit — but only after attempting the PRD-specified approach.

open_questions: Which specific Arabic/Latin calligraphic font will be used for the wordmark? PRD defers this; a decision is needed before the design-polish block to avoid a late-breaking layout reflow.

Should the 'niyet' wordmark render the Arabic نية as a visual glyph alongside the Latin word, or only the Latin? PRD says calligraphy is 'decoration, never content' but the header wordmark placement is unspecified.

What is the exact emerald gradient direction and stops? '135deg from #0a2e1c to #061a10' is a placeholder — needs sign-off against live screens before Block 4 (completion flow) locks in motion design on that background.
Is `background-attachment: fixed` desirable on mobile? On iOS Safari it can cause performance issues and rendering artefacts. Consider `background-attachment: scroll` for mobile via a media query.

Should `src/main.jsx` import `index.css`, or should `App.jsx` import it? Vite scaffold puts it in main.jsx; moving it to App.jsx is common in React patterns. Pick one convention now and document it to avoid double-imports in later blocks.
